NAME: DAVID
MANUAL_CITATION: Huang, D. W. et al. The DAVID Gene Functional Classification Tool: a novel biological module-centric algorithm to functionally analyze large gene lists. Genome Biol. 8, R183 (2007).
DESCRIPTION: Functional annotation and enrichment analysis platform.
FULL NAME: Database for Annotation, Visualization and Integrated Discovery
TITLE: The DAVID Gene Functional Classification Tool: a novel biological module-centric algorithm to functionally analyze large gene lists.
Authors: Huang DW,Sherman BT,Tan Q,Collins JR,Alvord WG,Roayaei J,Stephens R,Baseler MW,Lane HC,Lempicki RA
ABSTRACT: The DAVID Gene Functional Classification Tool http://david.abcc.ncifcrf.gov uses a novel agglomeration algorithm to condense a list of genes or associated biological terms into organized classes of related genes or biology, called biological modules. This organization is accomplished by mining the complex biological co-occurrences found in multiple sources of functional annotation. It is a powerful method to group functionally related genes and terms into a manageable number of biological modules for efficient interpretation of gene lists in a network context.
CITATION: Huang DW, Sherman BT, Tan Q, Collins JR, ...&, Lempicki RA. (2007) The DAVID Gene Functional Classification Tool: a novel biological module-centric algorithm to functionally analyze large gene lists. Genome Biol, 8 (9) R183. doi:10.1186/gb-2007-8-9-r183. PMID 17784955
